conference_context: SAGES 2019 - A View Through the Scope
subject_matter: Fellowship Council survey on minimally invasive surgical approaches in complex GI surgery

frame_analyses.notes: No financial conflicts disclosed by presenter

frame_analyses.content: Background on Fellowship Council accreditation and structure
Background on partner organizations (ASMBS, AHPBA, SSAT)
Methods - online survey design with sample question
frame_analyses.notes: Survey of 5-year alumni (2013-2017), n=835 email addresses

frame_analyses.content: Methods continued - survey question about bariatric cases
frame_analyses.notes: Shows Likert-scale survey instrument for case volume assessment
Defines minimally invasive approaches: laparoscopic, robotic, endoscopic
Breakdown by specialty: 193 Adv GI/MIS, 192 Bariatric, 48 HPB, 33 Flex Endo, 19 Colorectal, 11 Thoracic

frame_analyses.content: Use of MIS approaches - summary table
frame_analyses.notes: Shows 72%-100% MIS adoption across specialties; 4%-47% robotic; 8%-54% endoscopy

frame_analyses.content: Figure 2A - MIS case types among Advanced GI alumni
frame_analyses.notes: Bar chart showing case distribution across procedure types and volume categories

frame_analyses.content: Figure 2B - MIS cases among Advanced GI/MIS alumni
frame_analyses.notes: Similar breakdown for larger Advanced GI/MIS cohort (n=193)

frame_analyses.content: Figure 2C - MIS cases among Bariatric alumni
frame_analyses.notes: Case type distribution for bariatric surgery graduates (n=192)

frame_analyses.content: Figure 3E - Open cases among HPB alumni
frame_analyses.notes: Contrast showing HPB alumni perform predominantly open hepatopancreatobiliary cases

frame_analyses.content: Figure 2G - MIS cases among Thoracic alumni
frame_analyses.notes: Thoracic surgeons show high MIS adoption for foregut and thoracic cases

frame_analyses.content: Discussion - impact on prospective fellows and curriculum
frame_analyses.notes: Implications for career planning and curriculum building by PDs and organizations

scoring_determination: NOT SCORED - This is instructional/educational content
reason_not_scored: This video is a medical conference presentation (SAGES 2019) consisting of slides with research data, graphs, and discussion about fellowship training patterns. It is not a performance video of an FLS task. No trainee is performing any of the five FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). The content is entirely didactic with narrated research findings and should be classified as 'instructional' rather than a performance evaluation.

teaching_content.research_topic: Practice patterns in complex GI surgical fellowships regarding minimally invasive surgical approaches
key_findings: High adoption of MIS approaches across most fellowship types (72%-100% use >75% MIS)
Colorectal and Thoracic surgeons most likely to use MIS (100% and 91% respectively)
Bariatric surgeons have high MIS adoption (81%) with significant robotic component (20%)
HPB surgeons remain predominantly open (only 15% MIS >75% of practice)
Endoscopy integration highest in Flexible Endoscopy (54%) and Colorectal (48%) practices
curriculum_implications: Fellowship training must reflect current practice patterns in MIS adoption
Specialty-specific variation in approach (MIS vs open) requires targeted curriculum design
Robotic surgery training important for bariatric and colorectal fellowships
Endoscopic skills vary significantly by specialty and should be tailored accordingly
teaching_content.vertical_cues: Presenter discusses impact for prospective fellows and program directors in curriculum building
